paper.title: QUANT: A Minimalist Interval Method for Time Series Classification

claims.text: QUANT achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on 142 UCR datasets with less than 15 minutes total compute time using a single CPU core.
claims.evidence_snippet: QUANT achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on the expanded set of 142 datasets in the UCR archive with a total compute time (training and inference) of less than 15 minutes using a single CPU core.

tolerance.rationale: Full-archive runtime depends on CPU, dataset storage, and dependency versions. Public demo should use cached evidence or a reduced benchmark.

claims.text: QUANT uses quantile features, fixed intervals, and an off-the-shelf classifier to match the average accuracy of the most accurate interval methods.
claims.evidence_snippet: This distillation of interval-based approaches represents a fast and accurate method for time series classification.

tolerance.rationale: A single-dataset public demo can verify the pipeline and local metric, while full average-accuracy comparison should be cached or evaluated offline.

datasets.name: UCR Time Series Classification Archive
datasets.access_notes: Use only selected small datasets for public demo runs.

datasets.name: UCR Coffee dataset
datasets.access_notes: Candidate first dataset for a small end-to-end verification run.

manual_edits: Initial manifest drafted from arXiv metadata and official repository README.
Claims are marked needs_review until exact dataset, commands, and expected metrics are locked.

validation.notes: This draft is suitable for manifest loading, dashboard rendering, and later runner implementation.
